snapshot.readinessLevel: Moderate — good sleep, building back load

continue: Great recovery block — HRV weekly average climbed from 51ms (Jun 29) to 55-57ms this week, staying "Balanced" the whole way, while resting HR sits at a strong 45-48. The body is absorbing the volume rebound well.

This week's plan executed almost exactly as prescribed — easy runs at HR 124-132, VO2max intervals Wednesday, and a 9mi long run Saturday that still averaged just 132bpm despite being the longest run of the week.
ACWR is optimal at 0.9 (38%) — acute and chronic load are balanced, so there's genuine room to keep building rather than holding flat.
change: Polarized split reads 90% easy / 10% gray / 0% hard over the last 30 days by strict avg-HR — that looks great on paper, but it's partly an artifact of how averaging dilutes interval sessions (the Jul 15 VO2max day averaged only 146bpm across reps + recoveries, landing it in "gray"). The real signal is that easy days are staying genuinely under 135 individually, which they are.

Weekly mileage was choppy over the last month (18.7 → 11.1 → 4.5 → 23.1 mi) as tennis filled the gap during a busy travel/social stretch. Next week's ~25.5mi target continues the rebound at a controlled ~10% step rather than snapping straight back to peak volume.

VO2max is still flat around 52 for two months and training status flagged an "aerobic high" shortage (not enough tempo/threshold running relative to anaerobic work) — next week's quality day swaps to a tempo/cruise-interval session (3x6min @ ~7:00/mi) instead of repeating VO2max intervals, to target that specific gap.
